commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=60bdcc82731b28184b545aaabf33022f7bdd8c9e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WebChat follow lock so deliberate upward scrolling during streaming is preserved until the user returns to the bottom.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +35. Total +49 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source shows that the programmatic-scroll guard can preserve near-bottom follow after upward user movement, and the linked release report supplies concrete WebChat streaming steps.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Ask the contributor to broaden interruption detection, add small-delta coverage, and supply live browser proof; automation cannot provide proof from the contributor's real setup.

target.title: Fix WebChat backscroll during streaming
target.body:
Fixes #92386.

## Summary
- track an explicit WebChat follow lock when the user scrolls upward during streaming
- let deliberate upward input pass through the programmatic-scroll guard instead of being swallowed by a stale target
- keep auto-follow disabled until the user returns to the bottom, while preserving manual/always auto-scroll modes

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: WebChat no longer snaps a streaming conversation back to the bottom after the user deliberately scrolls upward near the bottom while a stale programmatic-scroll target is still active.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, head 60bdcc82731b28184b545aaabf33022f7bdd8c9e, Node/Vitest through the repository runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused WebChat scroll regression and adjacent chat suite with node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts, then ran pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/app-scroll.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts, git diff --check, and local child-mode pnpm check:changed.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the after-fix local OpenClaw run:

~~~text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw-92386-webchat-scroll-preserve
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  29 passed (29)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-ui.config.ts
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw-92386-webchat-scroll-preserve
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  86 passed (86)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 2.18s
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check -> no output
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core changed files
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The regression now records chatFollowLocked=true and chatUserNearBottom=false after a small upward scroll with a stale programmatic target, scheduleChatScroll leaves scrollTop at 1540 and sets the new-message indicator, and auto-follow only resumes after returning to the bottom.
- What was not tested: A live browser/gateway WebChat smoke was not run in this local pass.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
